Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: My argument, build more prisons, we need to have a consequence.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: We also need to...

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Not work. I've grown up in some of these communities

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: that you guys are talking about.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And prison is not a deterrent.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: If you don't change the environment, you won't change anything.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: And that's the key thing.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: You don't change the environment.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: This has been happening for years, okay?

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Years, yeah. And it's not always in the media, but it won't change.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: You need to...

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: A massive place for you to take it.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: It's going to take several things together.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: Yes, policing is one thing, but at the end of the day,

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: all of you guys on that side, you've benefited, yeah?

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: From the environment that you've grown up in, okay?

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: You've benefited from being in this work environment.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: I've benefited from it.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: But these boys, not all of them, get to benefit from the environment.

Speaker: SPEAKER 1
Text: If we don't show them something else, you won't change it.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: I was sat there in a way actually getting enraged,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: because it's quite hard for me as, you know,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: as a mixed race person that has grown up in some of the areas

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: that these boys come from, just to sit back and say nothing.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: It's easy to point the finger

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: when you've not actually lived that life

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: just throwing, stopping, search out there

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: as a way to fix it all.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: It's not the answer.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: What we see in the media is the end result.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: You don't see that person, that young boy,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: that young black boy as a child.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: It's like the media.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: We have a responsibility to portray

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: these stories correctly, you know,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: it's important to balance them

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and to also, you know, show the role models that are out there

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: because there's hundreds of young black boys,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: young black men that are positive role models in their community.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: But whenever we have these debates on television,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: you often find that the people that are being debated

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: aren't always represented on the program.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: In hindsight, it would be lovely

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: to have someone from the community come on

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and balance the argument

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and actually talk from experience.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: For me as a mixed race person, you know,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: my dad's black, you know,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: I feel very connected with my community.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: I can't just sit back and not say anything, you know.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: That's what I had to shout across the studio floor,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: especially when they were talking about building more prisons.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Prison isn't the answer.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: I've spoken to young boys, young men

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: that say that prison is not a deterrent,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: they're not scared of going to prison.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Don't put these boys into prison if you can help it.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: That's what I say.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: Teach them something else,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: show them a different way

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: because often that's when you lose young boys,

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: you know, into the system forever.

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: You know, they go into prison often

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: for doing something quite petty

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: and then they join up with other like-minded people

Speaker: SPEAKER 2
Text: that give them other ways in which they can commit crime.
